name: Tayba Foundation
mission:
Tayba aims to become a pioneer—the first to offer a complete, high-quality online curriculum featuring engaging visual and audio instruction Our programs SOURCE: Self-reported by organization What are the organization's current programs, how do they measure success,
            and who do the programs serve? Education At Tayba, we believe that character reformation gives incarcerated clients and the formerly incarcerated the tools they need to successfully reintegrate into society following parole.
theoryOfChange: Tayba Foundation states that through thoughtful, well-designed programs in Education, Life Skills, and Reentry, clients acquire tools needed to unlock human potential and forge a healthy future; mentoring relationships and continuing education are emphasized for successful reentry.
scoreSummary: TAYBA FOUNDATION shows exceptional impact and strong alignment, with zakat compliance.

all_citations.claim: The organization has a Charity Navigator score of 94.25/100, a program expense ratio of 84.6%, 6.7 months of working capital, and a fundraising efficiency of $0.18 per $1 raised.
The Tayba Foundation is Zakat-eligible and maintains specific policies regarding Zakat distribution.
The organization reported total revenue of $1,460,391 in its most recent financial filings.
Tayba offers an online curriculum and re-entry programs focused on character reformation and reducing recidivism.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a high overall score due to its exceptional financial health, transparency, and specialized focus on domestic poverty and prisoner rehabilitation.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing direct impact measurement to further validate the cost per beneficiary of approximately $5,469 [1].
Expanding donor alignment strategies to move beyond a medium fit for the target audience [1].

dimension_explanations.alignment: Tayba Foundation shows strong alignment with domestic poverty relief and is officially Zakat-eligible, adhering to specific distribution policies for asnaf categories [2]. It offers a medium donor fit for those seeking highly specialized domestic interventions and maintains high data confidence through verified financial filings [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates high impact through its capacity-building model, maintaining a Charity Navigator score of 94.25/100 and a program expense ratio of 84.6% [1]. While the cost per beneficiary is calculated at approximately $5,469, the organization's focus on non-recidivism provides a high-value societal return [4].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Tayba Foundation provides high-quality educational curricula and holistic re-entry support to empower incarcerated and formerly incarcerated individuals.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 84.6%, indicating a strong commitment to direct service delivery [1].
Maintains a healthy financial position with 6.7 months of working capital [1].
Strong fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.18 to raise every $1 [1].
baseline_narrative.summary: The Tayba Foundation is a non-profit organization dedicated to character reformation through a comprehensive online curriculum featuring visual and audio instruction [4]. With a focus on education, life skills, and re-entry services, the foundation aims to provide clients with the necessary tools to successfully reintegrate into society following parole [4].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support the spiritual and social rehabilitation of incarcerated Muslims in the US through zakat-eligible educational programs.
donor_motivations: Value the 'riqab' (freeing captives/helping the incarcerated) zakat category
Believe in education as the primary tool for character reformation
Want to address the specific needs of the American Muslim convert community in prisons
Prioritize high-accountability organizations with Platinum transparency seals
giving_considerations: The cost per student is high due to the specialized nature of prison correspondence
Recent revenue declines mean new donations are critical for maintaining current capacity
Impact is measured by long-term non-recidivism rather than immediate volume
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking international emergency relief or those looking for low-cost, high-volume interventions like meal distribution.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects <cite id="18">two tracked outcome metrics</cite> and a <cite id="7">verified 100% non-recidivism rate</cite>.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Tayba uses Islamic education and life skills to reform character, which directly leads to successful reentry and zero recidivism.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking recidivism ensures that educational programs are translating into real-world success for formerly incarcerated individuals.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The <cite id="4">recent 14% revenue decline</cite> suggests a significant need for new funding to sustain existing programs.

strengths.detail: The organization holds a <cite id="2">94.25/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="9">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.
Tayba directs a <cite id="10">84.6% program expense ratio</cite> toward its core services, ensuring the majority of funds reach students.
Internal data indicates a <cite id="7">100% non-recidivism rate</cite> for participants in their reentry programs.

strengths_deep_dive: Tayba Foundation excels in organizational culture and leadership, earning <cite id="11">perfect 100/100 scores in both categories</cite> from Charity Navigator. This internal strength supports a robust delivery model that includes <cite id="12">Islamic Education, Life Skills, and Reentry Support</cite>. Their financial health is further bolstered by <cite id="5">6.7 months of operating reserves</cite>, providing a significant buffer against the <cite id="4">recent 14% decline in revenue</cite>. Furthermore, the organization operates with an <cite id="13">independent board of 6 members</cite>, ensuring objective oversight of its mission-critical work in US prisons.
rich_narrative.summary: The Tayba Foundation operates as a specialized educational institution serving incarcerated and formerly incarcerated Muslims across the United States. The organization focuses on character reformation through a <cite id="1">comprehensive online curriculum featuring visual and audio instruction</cite>. By providing access to Islamic education, life skills, and reentry support, Tayba addresses the unique spiritual and practical needs of a highly underserved population. The foundation maintains a <cite id="2">94.25/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting high standards of accountability and leadership. While the organization manages <cite id="3">$1,460,391 in annual revenue</cite>, it recently faced a <cite id="4">14% revenue decline</cite> from the previous fiscal year. Despite this contraction, Tayba remains financially resilient with <cite id="5">6.7 months of working capital</cite> in reserve. Their model is particularly notable for its efficiency, requiring only <cite id="6">$0.18 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. Beyond education, Tayba engages in critical advocacy and research, contributing to a <cite id="7">100% non-recidivism rate</cite> among its tracked beneficiaries. This high-touch approach results in a <cite id="8">cost per beneficiary of approximately $5,469</cite>, which reflects the intensive nature of providing accredited correspondence courses and holistic reentry planning within the prison system.
